anzsic_codes.name: Other Heavy and Civil Engineering Construction
Engineering Design and Engineering Consulting Services

merger_description:
GenusPlus Group Limited (**Genus**) proposes to acquire 100% of the issued shares in MPC Kinetic Holdings Pty Ltd (**MPK**) through a Share Purchase Agreement.

Genus is an ASX-listed specialist power and communications infrastructure and services provider operating across Australia. Genus designs, builds and maintains electrical transmission and distribution networks, substations, rail and battery systems.

Genus’ business is split into three key divisions:

1. Infrastructure: designs, builds, and maintains high-voltage transmission and distribution networks (both overhead and underground), substations (terminal and zone), primary customer connections, and rail systems. Genus services electricity utilities, transmission operators, miners, and governments across metropolitan, regional, and remote Australia.
2. Energy and engineering: offers engineering, design, and procurement and construction solutions for electrical balance of plant (**EBoP**) services across communications and energy assets, with a focus on electrical systems such as Battery Energy Storage Systems (**BESS**) as well as grid integration.
3. Services: constructs and maintains fixed and wireless communication networks (including nbn fibre and Telstra contracts), provides asset management and upgrade services for power utilities, delivers vegetation management for powerline corridors, and operates digital workforce management platforms.

MPK provides specialised infrastructure and maintenance services to the energy and resources sectors in Australia and New Zealand.

MPK operates through three core business units:

1. Energy Infrastructure: installs gas gathering systems (civil infrastructure, pipeline and facilities installation and well head connection).
2. Renewables: civil balance of plant (**CBoP**) construction for onshore wind farms, civil construction (bulk earthworks and concrete works) for BESS, and mining civils (environmental earthworks and civil projects).
3. Energy Services: down-well maintenance and production supporting service lines - workover emissions reduction technology, integrity monitoring, well intervention services and well servicing. MPK also offers formulation and manufacturing of production chemicals in the oil and gas sector.

While both parties supply services that may be used by renewables projects, Genus specialises in the supply of EBoP services while MPK specialises in the supply of CBoP services. These are fundamentally different services, requiring different capabilities and skillsets. While MPK has some capability to supply some of the EBoP services (electrical reticulation and installation works) it does not presently supply any of these services.

Other services offered by Genus (e.g. telecommunication services, rail systems operations) and MPK (e.g. energy services in the gas industry) are non-overlapping and are not supplied in related industries to the operations of the other party.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
GenusPlus Group Limited (Genus) applied for a notification waiver in
respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued share
capital in MPC Kinetic Holdings Pty Ltd (MPK) through a Share
Purchase Agreement, as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Genus, is a supplier of power and communications
infrastructure services. Through its infrastructure division, Genus
designs, builds and maintains high-voltage transmission and
distribution networks, substations, primary customer connections
and rail systems. Through its energy and engineering division, it
offers engineering design, and procurement and construction
solutions for electrical balance of plant (EBoP) services to
communications and energy assets, with a focus on electrical
systems such as Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) and grid
integration. Finally, through its services division, it constructs and
maintains fixed and wireless communication networks (including
nbn fibre and Telstra contracts), provides asset management and
upgrade services for power utilities, delivers vegetation management
for powerline corridors, and operates digital workforce management
platforms.
The target, MPK, provides specialised infrastructure and
maintenance services to the energy and resources sectors in
Australia and New Zealand. It installs gas gathering systems,
supplies civil balance of plant (CBoP) construction for onshore wind
farms, civil construction for BESS and mining civils. It also supplies
down-well maintenance and production supporting service lines -
workover emissions reduction technology, integrity monitoring, well
intervention services and well servicing.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
• a. there is no horizontal overlap in the supply of CBoP services
in Australia
b. there is limited horizontal overlap in the supply of EBoP
services in Australia
c. while there may be a non-horizontal relationship between the
products and services supplied by Genus and MPK, there are
alternative suppliers of these products and services in
Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
